stories.title: Qualifying Final Day — Stephens Earns 14th Main Draw; Goffin Bids Farewell

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
David Goffin's Roland Garros career is over.

He lost in the qualifying final round today — then gave an emotional farewell speech: "I've always been welcomed here like a champion, like a prince."

15 years. 14 main draws. Au revoir, David. 🎾 #RolandGarros

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Sloane Stephens came back from a first-set loss to qualify for her 14th Roland Garros main draw.

The 2017 US Open champion is 35-13 lifetime in Paris. Still competing. Still winning. 🎾 #RolandGarros

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM ET

Two stories from the final day of Roland Garros qualifying — one that ends with a championship and one that ends with a speech.

David Goffin walked onto the qualifying court today for what turned out to be the last match of his Roland Garros career. The 35-year-old Belgian, who announced 2026 as his final year on tour, lost to Britain's Toby Samuel 5-7, 6-3, 6-3 in the final qualifying round. When it was over, he stayed on court and addressed the crowd on Suzanne-Lenglen with the grace that has defined his entire career.

"It's pure joy, nothing but emotion with some magnificent moments," Goffin said. "I've always been welcomed here like one of yours, like a champion, like a prince."

Nearly 15 years. 14 main draw appearances. Goffin's farewell to Roland Garros deserved this moment.

On the other side of the day's results: Sloane Stephens is back in the main draw. The 2017 US Open champion came back from dropping the first set against Spain's Leyre Romero Gormaz to secure her 14th Roland Garros main draw appearance. At 33, Stephens is 35-13 lifetime in Paris. She belongs here — and she proved it again today.

Bianca Andreescu and Karolina Pliskova were both eliminated in Wednesday's second qualifying round. The field for the 2026 main draw is now complete.

Main draw begins Sunday, May 24. Draw ceremony at 2 PM Paris today.

Which story moved you more today — Goffin's farewell or Stephens' comeback?

---

article.headline: Goffin's Roland Garros Career Ends in Tears as Stephens Books 14th Main Draw

stories.title: Roland Garros 2026 Draw Revealed — Brackets Set for Paris

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Roland Garros draw is set.

Women's top half: Sabalenka (No. 1) + Swiatek (No. 3).
Women's bottom half: Rybakina (No. 2) + Gauff (No. 4, defending).

Sabalenka and Rybakina can only meet in the final. #RolandGarros

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Men's draw is set at Roland Garros.

Sinner (No. 1) vs career Grand Slam.
Djokovic (No. 3) vs his own injury history.
Zverev (No. 2) vs everyone's expectations.

Main draw starts Sunday. 🎾 #RolandGarros

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:30 PM ET

The Roland Garros 2026 draw is set — and the brackets are full of intrigue.

Olympic legend Tony Estanguet placed all seeds at the ceremony today. Here is what it means:

WOMEN'S DRAW: Aryna Sabalenka (No. 1) and Iga Swiatek (No. 3) are in the same top half of the draw. If both progress, they meet in the semifinals. In the bottom half, Elena Rybakina (No. 2) and defending champion Coco Gauff (No. 4) share a quarter. Sabalenka and Rybakina — the two players who have dominated the WTA in 2026 — can only meet in the final. Elina Svitolina, the No. 7 seed and Rome champion, is seeded into the bracket.

MEN'S DRAW: Jannik Sinner headlines as No. 1 seed with the career Grand Slam on the line — Roland Garros is the only major missing from his collection. Novak Djokovic is No. 3, having played just one clay match all year. Alexander Zverev is No. 2 and is one of the few players capable of challenging Sinner over five sets on clay.

Main draw begins Sunday, May 24. Night sessions on Court Philippe-Chatrier from 8:15 PM each evening.

Prize money: €2.55 million for the singles champions.

Who are you picking to win Paris? Drop your final four in the comments.

---

stories.title: "Gaël & Friends" — Paris Bids Farewell to Monfils Tonight

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Tonight on Court Chatrier: Gael Monfils says goodbye to Roland Garros.

"Gaël & Friends" — 7:30 PM Paris. Sinner, Osaka, Svitolina, Gasquet, Tsonga. Music from Martin Solveig.

39 years old. Still the show. 🎾 #RolandGarros #Monfils

facebook.long_form:
Window: 4:00 PM ET

Tonight, Court Philippe-Chatrier is Gael Monfils' stage one last time.

"Gaël & Friends" begins at 7:30 PM Paris time — an evening of tennis, music, surprises, and above all, love for one of the most entertaining players Roland Garros has ever seen. The 39-year-old French legend, who has electrified Parisian crowds for two decades with his acrobatics and showmanship, gets the sendoff he deserves.

Who is joining him? On the tennis side: Jannik Sinner, Naomi Osaka, Elina Svitolina, Arthur Fils, Richard Gasquet, and Jo-Wilfried Tsonga. The music lineup features DJ Martin Solveig, rapper-dancer Franglish, and singer Matt Pokora.

This is not just a sporting event. It is a celebration. Proceeds from the evening go to Terre d'Impact (the FFT's fundraising foundation), KELINA, and the Epic Foundation.

If you have ever watched Monfils dive for a shot, hold a crowd in the palm of his hand, or simply play tennis with more joy than almost anyone else on tour — tonight is for you.

Watch live on the Roland Garros YouTube channel.

Are you watching tonight? Tell us your favourite Monfils moment in the comments.

---

stories.title: Wawrinka's Final Roland Garros — "There Will Be a Lot of Emotion"

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Stan Wawrinka on his final Roland Garros: "There will be a lot of emotion because it's Paris."

The 2015 champion bids farewell to Geneva, now heads to his 21st — and last — French Open. 🎾 #RolandGarros #Wawrinka

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 PM ET

Stan Wawrinka is coming home one last time.

The 41-year-old Swiss legend lost in the second round of the Geneva Open to Alex Michelsen 7-6, 7-6 this week — an emotional farewell to a home tournament where he won back-to-back titles in 2016 and 2017. When the match ended, career highlights played on the big screen at the Parc des Eaux-Vives. The crowd gave him the send-off he deserved. The screen read: "Merci Stan."

Now he shifts focus to Paris — and the 21st and final Roland Garros of his career.

Wawrinka won the French Open in 2015, defeating Novak Djokovic in the final in one of the greatest upsets in Grand Slam history. He also holds the 2014 Australian Open and 2016 US Open titles. Three Grand Slams, three jaw-dropping performances, and a legacy that will endure long past the last ball he strikes on Tour.

"There will be a lot of emotion because it's Paris and Roland-Garros," Wawrinka said. "I grew up with Roland-Garros, it's part of me. I hope I can win a few matches and above all enjoy the crowd."

He receives a wild card. He will be honoured after his final match.

Some careers end with records. Wawrinka's ends with something rarer: a crowd that never stopped believing in him.

---

stories.title: Roland Garros 2026 — What to Watch as Opening Week Begins Sunday

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Roland Garros 2026 is 3 days away.

Main draw: Sunday May 24.
Night sessions at Chatrier: 8:15 PM daily.
Sinner's career Grand Slam on the line.
Two legends (Monfils, Wawrinka) in their final Paris chapter.

Watch closely. 🎾 #RolandGarros

facebook.long_form:
Window: 6:00 PM ET

Roland Garros 2026 is three days away. Here is what you need to know before the first ball is struck on Sunday.

WHEN: Main draw begins Sunday, May 24. First-round matches run through Tuesday, May 26. Night sessions on Court Philippe-Chatrier from 8:15 PM. Finals: June 6 (women), June 7 (men).

THE BIG STORY — MEN'S: Jannik Sinner is chasing the career Grand Slam. He has won every other major, completed the Career Golden Masters, and carries a 29-match winning streak and five consecutive Masters 1000 titles into Paris. This is the one he has never won.

THE BIG STORY — WOMEN'S: Aryna Sabalenka is in the top half of the draw with Swiatek. She has never won Roland Garros — and she has to go through the woman who has won it four times to reach the final. Below them, Rybakina and defending champion Gauff are projected to meet in the other semifinal.

THE FAREWELL STORYLINES: Stan Wawrinka and Gael Monfils are both in their final Roland Garros. The crowd responses when each of them play will be among the most memorable moments of the tournament.

THE WILDCARD: Novak Djokovic. One clay match all year. Three Paris titles. You know never to count him out.

This tournament is three days away. Are you ready?

---
